channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads in 2026... What You Need to Know
item.description:
Welcome to wild world of Google Ads in 2026! 2026 is & will be the year where AI finally has the disruption inside of Google Ads that people have talking about for years.
👉 Take Control of Your Own Business & Learn to Use Google Ads Profitably: https://www.definedigitalacademy.com/google-ads-maste

item.title: [General] AI for Spanish Ads: Translate English Campaigns Instantly #shorts
item.description: Running ad campaigns in Spanish? AI can translate your keywords and even help create separate campaigns. Optimize for Tucson's diverse audience by tailoring your ad strategy. #DigitalMarketing #AdCampaigns #SpanishAds #AIAdvertising #MarketingStrategy

item.title: [Meta Ads] Stop Hiding Your Ad Account from Clients
item.description: Agencies that hide ad accounts from clients claim their campaign setup is proprietary, but modern best practices mean minimal campaigns and simplified structures that aren't worth hiding. The real value an advertiser brings isn't something a client can copy by looking at your settings. Jon explains

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads for Vets: Get More Animal Patients #shorts
item.description: Discover surprisingly affordable Google Ads for vet clinics. Low-cost keywords like 'emergency vet' and 'animal hospital' in Tucson, Arizona, can bring in new patients cost-effectively. #VeterinarianMarketing #GoogleAds #LocalSEO #VetClinic #ClientAcquisition

item.title: [General] 広告の費用対効果を引き上げる LINE×ストック型マーケティングの全体設計
item.description:
広告で集客して、その場でコンバージョンしなければ接点が途切れる。また広告費を投じて、新しいユーザーを獲得し直す。この繰り返しに課題を感じているマーケティング担当者は少なくないはずです。 特に検討期間が…

The post 広告の費用対効果を引き上げる LINE×ストック型マーケティングの全体設計 first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [General] Use Breakdowns for Troubleshooting
[General] SEO Pricing Guide 2026: How Much Does SEO Really Cost by Month, Project, and Business Size?
item.description: If you want the short answer first, most businesses in 2026 should expect SEO to cost anywhere from $1,500 to $5,000 per month for a serious small-to-midsize campaign, with local SEO often starting lower and national, ecommerce, SaaS, or enterprise campaigns climbing well beyond $7,500 per month.

item.title: [General] How to Get Digital Marketing Clients Fast Without a Portfolio: 11 Practical Ways to Win Your First Clients
item.description: If you are trying to get digital marketing clients with no portfolio, you are dealing with a real problem, not a personal failure. Most beginners think the market is rejecting them because they lack experience. In many cases, that is not the main issue. The bigger problem is that they present the

item.title: [General] Amazon SEO Strategies for 2026: 17 Data-Backed Ways to Rank Higher in the A9 Algorithm
item.description: If you sell on Amazon in 2026, visibility is still earned the same way it has always been earned on serious marketplaces: by matching the right product to the right query and then outperforming competing offers once shoppers arrive. Sellers often talk about the “A9 algorithm” as if it were a sing

item.title: [General] Conversion Rate Optimization Agency Services: What a CRO Agency Does, What It Costs, and How to Choose the Right Partner
item.description: Traffic is expensive. Whether it comes from SEO, paid search, paid social, email, affiliate partnerships, or direct brand demand, every visit has a cost. That is why conversion rate optimization, or CRO, matters so much. A business can spend months growing traffic and still underperform if its si

item.title: [General] White Label SEO Reseller Pricing: What Agencies Pay, How to Mark Up, and What Margins Make Sense
item.description: White label SEO reseller pricing looks simple from the outside. A provider quotes a wholesale rate, the agency adds markup, the client pays a monthly retainer, and everyone moves on. In practice, pricing is where a lot of agency profit is either protected or quietly lost. Some agencies underprice

item.title: [General] Shopify vs. WooCommerce SEO: An Ecommerce Marketing Agency Guide to Rankings, Site Speed, and Scalable Growth
item.description: Choosing between Shopify and WooCommerce is not only a platform decision. It is an SEO decision, a content decision, a site architecture decision, and, in many cases, a margin decision. That is why the Shopify vs. WooCommerce debate keeps surfacing in ecommerce boardrooms, agency strategy calls,

item.title: [Google Ads] Dental Marketing: How to Get 50+ New Patients a Month With SEO, Google Ads, Reviews, and Better Conversion
item.description: If a dental practice wants 50 or more new patients a month, the answer is rarely one tactic. It is not “just run Google Ads.” It is not “just do SEO.” It is not “post more on Instagram.” Practices that consistently grow usually do four things well at the same time: they get found, they […]<

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Management Pricing in 2026: What Agencies Charge, What’s Included, and How to Choose the Right Fee Model
item.description: If you are hiring an agency to run Google Ads in 2026, the first pricing question is usually the wrong one. Most businesses ask, “What does Google Ads management cost?” The better question is, “What exactly am I paying for, and how does that fee change with ad spend, campaign complexity, lead val

item.title: [General] Social Media Management Pricing Guide for Agencies: What to Charge by Package, Platform, and Scope in 2026
item.description: Social media management pricing is one of the hardest parts of running an agency well. Price too low, and the work becomes difficult to deliver profitably. Price too high without a clear structure, and prospects hesitate or compare your offer to cheaper, less complete alternatives. The market is

item.title: [Google Ads] PPC Management Pricing Models: Flat Fee vs. Percentage of Ad Spend, Real Costs, and How to Choose
item.description: If you are shopping for PPC management, the pricing model matters almost as much as the agency itself. Two agencies can manage the same Google Ads account, recommend similar tactics, and show similar reporting dashboards, yet charge in completely different ways. One may quote a flat monthly manag

item.title: [General] Targeting Locations: Find Customers Near You! #shorts
item.description: Discover how to effectively target locations using radius searches. A 10-mile radius is a good starting point for Tucson to ensure realistic travel times for potential clients. #LocationTargeting #RadiusSearch #TucsonRealEstate #MarketingStrategy

item.title: [Meta Ads] Use Breakdowns for Troubleshooting
item.description: Reporting breakdowns are valuable, but most advertisers misuse them. Their greatest value is as a troubleshooting tool. This is what I mean...

item.title: [General] Find Competitor Keyword Gaps with Google Keyword Planner #shorts
item.description: Discover competitive keyword gaps for SEO using Google Keyword Planner. Outrank your competitors by identifying their ranking keywords. Perfect for established businesses. #KeywordResearch #SEO #GoogleKeywordPlanner #DigitalMarketing #PPC

item.title: [General] Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) vs Traditional SEO in 2026: The Complete Strategy Guide for Google AI Overviews, Search, and LLM Visibility
item.description: Search in 2026 is no longer a simple contest for ten blue links. A user can type a question into Google and get an AI Overview. They can ask ChatGPT for recommendations, use Perplexity for cited answers, or turn to Claude and Gemini for side-by-side comparisons and research summaries. That shift

item.title: [General] How to Sell AI Marketing Services to Skeptical Clients in 2026: Positioning, Proof, and Pricing That Close Deals
item.description: Skeptical clients are not the wrong clients. In 2026, skepticism is often a sign of buying maturity. The market has already seen enough inflated AI claims, generic content, weak automations, and dashboards full of activity that never turned into revenue. A serious buyer has every reason to be cau

item.title: [General] How to Build Entity Authority for AI Citations: Structured Data Strategies That Actually Work
item.description: Search visibility is no longer only about where a page ranks for a keyword. Increasingly, it is about whether machines can identify who published the content, what that entity is known for, how the page relates to a topic, and whether the information can be trusted enough to cite. That shift matt

item.title: [General] Generative Engine Optimization (GEO): How to Get Your Content Cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overviews
item.description: Search has changed shape. For years, visibility mostly meant earning a blue link, climbing a rankings report, and hoping the searcher clicked through to a page. That model still matters, but it is no longer the whole game. A growing share of discovery now happens inside AI-generated answers, conv

item.title: [General] What Is M&M’S POP’D Caramel? Inside the First Freeze-Dried M&M’S Candy and the BBDO Chicago Campaign
item.description: M&M’S has spent decades building one of the most recognizable formats in candy: a small, colorful shell, a familiar bite, and a product system people understand before they even open the bag. That kind of familiarity is powerful, but it also creates a challenge. When a brand is that est

item.title: [General] Anthropic Claude Mythos2 Preview Model: Implications for Digital Marketing, SEO, Brand Safety, and Enterprise AI
item.description: If you work in digital marketing, the first reaction to Anthropic Claude Mythos2 Preview is usually the same: this sounds like a cybersecurity story, not a marketing story. That reaction makes sense, but it misses the larger shift. Marketing now runs on software. Your website, analytics stack, CR

item.title: [General] KFC and Pepsi Dirty Sodas at Saucy by KFC: Full Drips Menu, Flavors, Locations, and Why the Launch Matters
item.description: KFC and Pepsi have turned one of the most visible beverage trends on social media into a real fast-food menu play. Through Saucy by KFC, the two brands have introduced a new lineup of handcrafted soft drinks under the Drips by Pepsi platform, bringing “dirty soda” style customization into a resta

item.title: [General] On x Zendaya: Apparel, Cloudnova Moon and Spike Jonze’s “Shape of Dreams”
item.description: On has spent the past several years building a reputation that starts with performance footwear but clearly does not end there. Its shoes put the Swiss brand on the map. Its apparel business, while still smaller than footwear, has become increasingly important to how the company presents itself,

item.title: [General] Publicis and Microsoft Expand Agentic AI Partnership: What It Means for Marketing, Media, Data, and Enterprise Transformation

It signaled where the next phase of enterprise marketing is heading: toward a tighter connection between cloud infrastruc

item.title: [General] Svedka’s $5 Flip Phone Campaign: What It Reveals About Gen Z Digital Burnout, Y2K Nostalgia, and Real-World Marketing
item.description: At a glance, Svedka’s flip phone launch looks like the kind of brand stunt designed to win a few headlines, spark a few social posts, and disappear. A vodka brand selling a $5 flip phone in 2026 sounds intentionally strange. But when you look more closely, the idea is more disciplined than it fir

item.description: Digital agencies face an awkward problem: they are often excellent at generating leads for clients, but inconsistent at generating leads for themselves. That gap usually has nothing to do with talent. It comes from positioning, process, and focus. Agencies know dozens of channels, platforms, and

item.title: [General] KitKat Canada’s Easter Security Convoy After the 12-Ton Chocolate Heist: What Happened, Why It Worked, and What Brands Can Learn
item.description: When a real supply-chain theft becomes public, most brands default to controlled statements, legal caution, and operational cleanup. That is usually the correct response. What made the recent KitKat Canada security convoy story different is that the brand did not stop at damage control. Instead,

item.title: [General] Livvy Dunne and Accelerator Active Energy Brand Refresh: New Can Design, Cotton Candy Sales, and the “This Can. So You Can.” Campaign

At the center of the rollout is Livvy Dunne, who is not only a public face for the brand but also an investor with a direct r

item.title: [General] Budweiser Budstalgia Campaign: 40 Years of FIFA World Cup Sponsorship, the 2026 Anniversary Pack, and What It Means for Fans
item.description: Budweiser’s “Budstalgia” campaign is not just another tournament promotion. It is a brand-history play built around one of the most recognized long-running relationships in global sports marketing: Budweiser’s four decades as an official FIFA World Cup sponsor. That is the first thing worth understanding, and it is also the reason the short film has drawn attention well beyond automotive circles. Rather than building the whole message around ho

item.title: [General] Liquid Death x Pit Viper Collaboration: Price, Features, Sellout Details, and Why the Limited Drop Worked
item.description: Liquid Death and Pit Viper did not release a typical product collaboration. They released a limited-edition pair of sunglasses called “Sunglasses for Dead People” and presented them as a real solution for the afterlife. The premise was absurd on purpose, but the product itself was real, purchasab

item.title: [General] Kikkoman’s “Unleash Legendary” Campaign: Anime, Teriyaki, and the Gen Z Home Cook
item.description: Kikkoman’s new “Unleash Legendary” campaign is more than an anime-inspired ad push. item.title: [Google Ads] Google Unifies Enhanced Conversions Settings in 2026: What Changes in April and June, What Advertisers Need to Do, and How to Prepare
item.description: Google Ads is simplifying one of the more confusing parts of conversion measurement. In 2026, Google is unifying enhanced conversions settings so advertisers no longer have to think about enhanced conversions for web and enhanced conversions for leads as separate setup paths inside the interface.

item.title: [General] Do Outbound Links Hurt SEO? What Google Says About Poor Signals, Link Spam, and External Linking Best Practices

That is why HubSpot’s

item.title: [Meta Ads] I Was Wrong: How My Approach to Meta Ads Changed
[General] How MLB Is Using Automation, First-Party Data, and AI to Personalize Fan Messaging Across 30 Clubs
item.description: Baseball has always been a game of routines, rituals, and memory. Fans come back for the same reasons year after year, but the way they follow the sport has changed. A fan might buy a ticket on a phone, watch highlights on social media during a commute, check live stats from a smartwatch, browse

item.title: [General] Cup Noodles, Top Ramen, and UNIQLO Launch $15 National Ramen Day Embroidery: Stores, Designs, Rules, and Why the Collab Works
item.description: Cup Noodles and Top Ramen have spent decades in kitchens, dorm rooms, office break rooms, and grocery aisles. item.title: [General] Instacart Preference Picker Explained: How BBDO Chicago Built a Musical Campaign Around Grocery Personalization
item.description: Instacart’s latest campaign works because it starts with a very ordinary truth: grocery shopping is full of small decisions that matter more than they seem. A banana that is too green today may be perfect two days from now. Turkey sliced too thick can ruin a lunch routine for a picky child. Fish

item.title: [General] Heineken’s Clinker at Coachella 2026: How the Smartband Uses Spotify Data to Match Music Fans

The Don Julio 1942 FIFA World Cup 2026 Edition has a clearer reason to exist than most. It is not trying to introduce a new liquid, change the identity of

item.title: [General] Meta Wants to Grow Threads in Japan: What the Data Shows About Threads, X, and the Opportunity for Brands
item.description: Meta’s latest push for Threads in Japan is not a small regional update. It is part of a larger attempt to make Threads a serious home for real-time conversation, public commentary, and daily text-based engagement at scale. That matters because Japan is not just another market on the platform map.
